description: Producing or able to produce large amounts of goods, crops, or results. 'Poru' names the quality of effective output—work that yields results, processes that generate goods, efforts that bear fruit.
sound_symbolism: The word produces steadily. 'Po' begins with purposeful release, output initiated. Then 'ru' rolls with continuous yield, production flowing.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'poru thiloma' (productive garden). Related to 'muroa' (fertile) but emphasizes actual output rather than potential—productive work has already yielded results.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Productive systems create what communities need—production aligned with genuine needs rather than artificial demand.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Productive seasons determined survival—the rhythm of productive and fallow times structured traditional life.
